authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification v3.2.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for describing HTTP API contracts, operations, parameters, schemas, examples, and security schemes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract artifact assembly and review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, request, response, status, representation, and protocol semantics behavior.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 3986: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI): Generic Syntax
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 3986 defines URI syntax and resolution concepts used for API resource identifiers, paths, queries, and links.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9457: Problem Details for HTTP APIs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9457 is the current standard for machine-readable HTTP API problem detail responses and replaces the older RFC 7807 baseline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API error model and validation review
practical_use_in_pack: contract tests for consistent failure handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9700: Best Current Practice for OAuth 2.0 Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9700 is Best Current Practice for OAuth 2.0 security and directly constrains API authorization guidance that affects scopes, token handling, and client-facing contract behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API authorization contract and security review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, OAuth working group update, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP API Security Top 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 is a recognized public taxonomy of API-specific security risks for exposed API surfaces.
practical_use_in_pack: resource consumption and unsafe API consumption risks
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP API Security edition update.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national framework for secure software development practices, secure implementation evidence, vulnerability response, and organizational controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure development evidence review and remediation tracking
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST update, revision, or related federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: Google API Improvement Proposals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google publishes public AIPs for API design, resource modeling, standard methods, compatibility, pagination, and lifecycle guidance used in large API programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resource modeling, lifecycle, and client-delivery review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Google publishes material AIP updates relevant to resource design or compatibility.

authoritative_sources.title: Microsoft REST API Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes production-oriented REST API guidelines for consistent API style, versioning, errors, pagination, and client compatibility in a major platform ecosystem.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API consistency, versioning, and client compatibility review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Microsoft updates the repository guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: ASP.NET Core Web API and OpenAPI documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn publishes official ASP.NET Core Web API and OpenAPI documentation for a common enterprise API runtime and contract-generation path.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: runtime-specific API contract generation and evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when ASP.NET Core versioned documentation changes API/OpenAPI behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions for HTTP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTelemetry publishes vendor-neutral telemetry specifications and HTTP semantic conventions used for API traces, metrics, logs, and observability evidence.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OpenTelemetry semantic convention stability changes.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: About Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines workflow evidence and CI behavior for repositories that validate API contracts and release gates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI evidence collection and quality-gate review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About Pull Request Reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official pull request review documentation defines review comments, requested changes, approvals, and review evidence in a common software delivery system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract review, finding status, and resubmission tracking
practical_use_in_pack: distinction between review support and approval authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, professional judgment, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, and duty boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, client delivery, and professional judgment boundary
practical_use_in_pack: refusal to overstate authority or expose tenant data
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ACM/IEEE Computer Society code is a recognized professional framework for software engineers, including public interest, client and employer obligations, product quality, judgment, management, and profession boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional-services quality, client commitment, and authority boundary
practical_use_in_pack: boundary between technical support and final approval
scenario coverage for client rights and professional judgment
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM/IEEE Computer Society code revision.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm API task type, tenant facts, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing the request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: API artifact and criteria review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect requirements, API contracts, source schemas, examples, dependency contracts, gateway constraints, tests, logs, metrics, and review notes against API correctness, interoperability, security, maintainability, compatibility, and lifecycle criteria.
source_classes: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: finding classification and recommendation drafting
workflow_stages.description: Convert evidence into API surface maps, OpenAPI recommendations, contract review reports, guardrail lists, compatibility findings, or escalation packages with clear assumptions, severity, owners, and confidence limits.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: remediation and resubmission tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track open findings, remediation owners, updated contract diffs, example updates, contract tests, compatibility checks, reviewer responses, and resubmission records without closing unsupported work.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: delivery, confidentiality, and signoff gating
workflow_stages.description: Before packaging a review-ready output, check recipient authorization, confidentiality, tenant policy, contractual scope, release approval, security ownership, platform ownership, and professional judgment boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: business requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, domain glossary, API consumers, non-functional requirements, tenant policy, confidentiality terms, and approval matrix
OpenAPI descriptions, API contracts, source schemas, examples, dependency contracts, client SDK assumptions, developer-portal content, service catalog entries, and contract tests
platform/API gateway limits, rate-limit policy, authentication and authorization requirements, data classification records, security requirements, privacy requirements, and SLA/SLO targets
contract diffs, review comments, compatibility matrices, deprecation plans, migration plans, rollback plans, release checklists, and reviewer responses
logs, traces, metrics, dashboards, request IDs, error samples, incident records, CI workflow definitions, build outputs, and test reports

API surface boundary map, path-by-path design, OpenAPI-first recommendation, contract review report, guardrail list, review or inspection summary, evidence checklist, remediation note, and escalation or refusal note
systems_of_record: source repository and pull request review system
API contract registry, OpenAPI repository, schema registry, event catalog, service catalog, or developer portal
issue tracker, API design review tracker, remediation tracker, deprecation tracker, and review-note register
CI/CD system, contract test report store, build artifact store, and release checklist
observability platform for logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, request identifiers, and SLO evidence
identity and access policy records, data classification records, privacy/security requirements, and rate-limit policy
tenant document management system, engagement scope, confidentiality records, approved recipients, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend API resource models, paths, methods, request and response shapes, examples, schema constraints, error models, versioning plans, compatibility mitigations, and contract tests
classify API evidence as satisfied, unclear, failed, or blocked for design and review support
prepare review summaries, evidence checklists, remediation notes, guardrail lists, and escalation packages tied to named artifacts
route adjacent or authority-bound work to the correct owner
escalate: release approval, merge approval, contractual acceptance, legal interpretation, pricing, client rights, privacy approval, or licensed/professional judgment is required
tenant policy conflicts with public source guidance or confidentiality terms change the allowed output
critical API contract, source schema, dependency contract, CI, log, metric, incident, or approval evidence is missing or contradictory
security risk acceptance, threat model ownership, platform operations, API gateway operations, database ownership, or implementation execution is required
refuse: requests for full frontend implementation, backend handler implementation, physical database schema ownership, Terraform, CI/CD ownership, infrastructure scaling, cloud migration, or network operations
requests for legal advice, pricing strategy, tenant-specific contract terms, editorial approval, release approval, attestation, or final signoff
requests to expose confidential tenant/client API contracts, payload examples, logs, or client commitments to unapproved recipients
requests to treat API design review as a substitute for security risk acceptance, privacy approval, platform approval, or contractual approval

domain_failure_modes: generic API advice that is not tied to the provided requirements, API contract, source schemas, examples, tests, logs, or tenant systems of record
unsupported closure when source schemas, contract tests, compatibility evidence, remediation proof, or reviewer responses are missing
outdated Problem Details or OpenAPI assumptions used despite current authoritative sources changing the baseline
API design recommendations widen into frontend implementation, backend code ownership, database schema execution, gateway operations, CI/CD ownership, Terraform, or platform operations
security guidance turns into threat-model ownership, risk acceptance, or security policy approval
confidential client API designs, payload examples, logs, or tenant commitments are sent to an unapproved recipient
legal, pricing, tenant contract, client rights, privacy, release, or final approval determinations are implied by technical API review language
tenant-specific policy conflicts are confidently resolved without approval owner review
runtime guidance assumes privileged credentials, secrets, production access, or tenant-specific facts not supplied

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
security and quality expectations where applicable
Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent.
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority.
client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific API style and framework precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The exact naming, pagination, versioning, and OpenAPI generation conventions may differ by tenant, runtime, and API program.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack uses public API sources and records that tenant-supplied style guides, runtime choices, and policy details must be retrieved before stack-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, contractual, privacy, release, and security authority
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Technical API review can inform but cannot decide client rights, contractual duties, pricing, legal notice wording, privacy approval, risk acceptance, or release signoff.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to human owners or orchestrator.

notes: The source mix covers API contract standards, protocol semantics, URI syntax, current Problem Details guidance, OAuth security, API-specific security, secure software lifecycle guidance, client-delivery workflow, CI evidence, runtime documentation, observability terminology, professional ethics, confidentiality, and failure handling.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because each open point is converted into tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, or orchestrator-return rules.
